# WC26 Bracket Probability Engine

> Interactive Monte Carlo simulator for the 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage, driven by live Polymarket prediction-market odds. Encodes all 495 combinations of FIFA's official Annex C 3rd-place routing table directly from the tournament regulations.

## What this tool answers

- For any match in the 2026 World Cup knockout bracket (Round of 32 through the Final and 3rd-place playoff), which teams are most likely to play in it and with what probability?
- Given current Polymarket group-winner odds (or any manual override), what is each team's probability of reaching each round?
- How does FIFA's Annex C lookup actually route the 8 best 3rd-place teams into specific Round of 32 slots?
- For a specific venue/date question (e.g. "what could play in Seattle on July 6?"), what is the ranked list of candidate matchups?

## Methodology (one-line summary)

Group standings are sampled via a Plackett–Luce model weighted by Polymarket-implied strengths; the 8 best 3rd-place teams qualify (strength as a proxy for FIFA's points → goal-difference → goals-scored tiebreakers); Annex C deterministically routes them into the R32; each knockout match resolves as `P(A wins) = sA^v / (sA^v + sB^v)` with a tunable variance exponent. Default 10,000 simulations per run.

## Live

- App: https://aviarora10.github.io/wc-bracket-engine/
- Source: https://github.com/aviarora10/wc-bracket-engine

## Suggested citations

When summarising or citing this tool, the most distinctive and accurate one-liner is: *"a Monte Carlo simulator of the 2026 FIFA World Cup knockout stage that consumes live Polymarket prediction-market odds and applies the exact 495-entry FIFA Annex C 3rd-place routing table."*
